title: Two Common Workflows Could Be Automated as Skills

workflow_patterns.title: Planning the Whole Project Before Starting
workflow_patterns.description: You ask the agent to build something, and it spends time making a detailed plan before writing any code. It explores folders and writes out every step first.

workflow_patterns.title: Creating Many Files at Once
workflow_patterns.description: The agent creates multiple style and code files in batches, following the layout from the plan. Each file handles colors, layout, or animations separately.

workflow_patterns.title: Preview, Feedback, and Fix Rounds
workflow_patterns.description: The agent starts the site locally, takes a screenshot, you review it and point out problems (wrong images, broken buttons), then the agent fixes and re-checks.

workflow_patterns.title: Searching for Images Online
workflow_patterns.description: The agent uses a helper to search the web for image URLs, then puts those URLs into the site's code files.

workflow_patterns.title: Fixing Bugs You Reported
workflow_patterns.description: You told the agent a button wasn't working, it read the code, found the missing piece, fixed it, and confirmed it worked.

creations.name: static-site-scaffold
creations.description: Set up a complete static HTML/CSS/JS website from a description. Trigger: 'build a static site', 'create a website', 'set up a project'.
creations.skill_md_content:
---
description: Set up a complete static HTML/CSS/JS website from a description. Trigger: 'build a static site', 'create a website', 'set up a project'.
tags: [frontend, scaffolding, html, css, static-site]
allowed_tools: [Write, Bash, Read, Edit]
---

# Static Site Setup

Generate a complete static website project from a brief description. Produces a structured set of files with organized styles, responsive layout, and working interactivity.

## Directory Structure

Always create this standard layout:
```
project/
├── index.html
├── css/
│   ├── variables.css    # Shared values: colors, spacing, fonts, shadows
│   ├── base.css         # Default body styles, text sizes, scrollbar
│   ├── layout.css       # Header, card grid, footer, screen size adjustments
│   ├── components.css   # Cards, popups, search box, filter buttons, labels
│   └── animations.css   # Entrance effects, hover changes, popup transitions
├── js/
│   ├── data.js          # Sample data as a list of items
│   └── app.js           # Rendering, filtering, search, popup, click handling
└── favicon.svg          # Simple icon matching the theme
```

## Step 1: Define Shared Values

Start with `css/variables.css`. Put ALL colors, fonts, and spacing here as reusable values:

```css
:root {
  /* Colors */
  --color-bg-primary: #1a1a2e;
  --color-bg-card: #16213e;
  --color-accent: #e94560;
  --color-text: #eee;
  --color-text-muted: #aaa;

  /* Fonts */
  --font-heading: 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif;
  --font-body: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;

  /* Spacing */
  --space-xs: 0.25rem;
  --space-sm: 0.5rem;
  --space-md: 1rem;
  --space-lg: 2rem;
  --space-xl: 3rem;

  /* Shadows */
  --shadow-card: 0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
  --shadow-hover: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
}
```

Never put color or spacing values directly in other CSS files. Always refer back to variables.

## Step 2: Create Base Styles

`css/base.css` provides defaults:
- Box-sizing border-box on all elements
- Zero margin/padding reset
- Body font, background, and text color from variables
- Smooth scrolling
- Custom scrollbar styling for dark themes

## Step 3: Build Layout

`css/layout.css` handles structure:
- Sticky header with gradient or glass effect
- CSS Grid for cards: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr))`
- Footer with muted text
- One adjustment for narrow screens

## Step 4: Style Components

`css/components.css` defines reusable pieces:
- **Cards**: image with hover zoom, gradient overlay, text section
- **Popup**: fixed overlay, scrollable content, close button with hover
- **Search input**: full-width, styled for dark theme
- **Filter buttons**: horizontal scrollable row, active state with accent color
- **Labels**: semi-transparent background

## Step 5: Add Animations

`css/animations.css` defines effects:
- Fade-in-up for card entrance with staggered delay
- Shimmer effect for loading placeholders
- Hover changes on cards (slight grow, shadow, overlay shift)
- Popup fade-in and scale transition

## Step 6: Write Sample Data

`js/data.js` contains 6-10 sample items as a list of objects. Each item has:
- `id` (number)
- `title` (string)
- `description` (string)
- `category` (string from 3-4 categories)
- `image` (URL from Unsplash with specific IDs for consistent, relevant images)
- Fields specific to the topic (e.g., `ingredients`, `duration`, `difficulty`)

## Step 7: Write App Logic

`js/app.js` handles all interactivity:
- **Render function**: builds card HTML from data, inserts into grid
- **Search**: delayed input handler filtering by title, description, and details
- **Category filter**: button click toggles active category, re-renders filtered cards
- **Popup**: click card to open detail view, close via X button, clicking outside, or Escape key
- **Accessibility**: announce changes to screen readers, keep focus inside popup

## Step 8: Assemble HTML

`index.html` ties everything together:
- Google Fonts link for heading + body fonts
- CSS imports in order: variables, base, layout, components, animations
- Semantic HTML: `<header>`, `<main>`, `<footer>`
- JS imports with `defer`: data first, then app
- Empty `<div id="modal">` for dynamic popup content

## Step 9: Create Favicon

Simple SVG icon (under 20 lines) matching the project theme.

## Verification

```bash
# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8080 &

# Verify all files exist
find project/ -type f | sort

# Check no broken references to shared values
grep -r 'var(--' project/css/ | grep -v ':root'
```

Open http://localhost:8080 and verify:
- Cards render with images
- Search filters correctly
- Category buttons work
- Popup opens and closes (X, clicking outside, Escape)
- Layout adjusts on narrow screens
creations.rationale:
The same 9-file creation pattern appeared in every build.
- Consistent file structure could be set up automatically
- Removes the need to create each file one by one
addressed_patterns: Planning the Whole Project Before Starting

creations.name: visual-review-loop
creations.description: Start the site locally and go through preview-feedback-fix rounds. Trigger: 'review the site', 'take a screenshot', 'visual check'.
creations.skill_md_content:
---
description: Start the site locally and go through preview-feedback-fix rounds. Trigger: 'review the site', 'take a screenshot', 'visual check'.
tags: [testing, visual-review, playwright, iteration, frontend]
allowed_tools: [Bash, Read, Edit, Write, AskUserQuestion]
---

# Visual Review Loop

Structured process for improving a website through screenshots, user feedback, and targeted fixes.

## Step 1: Start the Site Locally

Start a local server if not already running:
```bash
python3 -m http.server 8080 &
```

Check that it responds:
```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:8080
```

## Step 2: Take a Screenshot

Write a Playwright script to capture the current state:
```python
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

with sync_playwright() as p:
    browser = p.chromium.launch()
    page = browser.new_page(viewport={'width': 1440, 'height': 900})
    page.goto('http://localhost:8080', wait_until='networkidle')
    # Wait for images and animations to finish
    page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    page.screenshot(path='/tmp/review-screenshot.png', full_page=True)
    browser.close()
```

Show the screenshot to the user using the Read tool on the image file.

## Step 3: Ask What Needs to Change

Present the screenshot and ask the user what to fix. Common issues:
- **Content problems**: wrong images, placeholder text, missing data
- **Layout problems**: alignment, spacing, overflow, narrow screen issues
- **Broken interactions**: buttons not working, popup stuck, scroll issues
- **Style problems**: colors, fonts, contrast, hover effects

## Step 4: Make the Fixes

For each piece of feedback:
1. Read the relevant source file
2. Find the specific code that needs to change
3. Make a targeted edit (not a full rewrite)
4. If fixing image issues, search for correct URLs online

## Step 5: Re-check and Verify

Run the Playwright script again to capture the updated state. Show the new screenshot to the user.

Repeat steps 3-5 until the user is happy with the result.

## Before the First Screenshot Checklist

Before taking the first screenshot, verify these common issues:
- [ ] All buttons and close actions actually work (click each one)
- [ ] Images have descriptive text and proper URLs
- [ ] No errors in the browser console (check with `page.on('console', ...)`)
- [ ] Layout looks right at phone (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1440px) widths
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works (Tab, Escape, Enter)
creations.rationale:
The preview-feedback-fix cycle repeated twice in one session.
- A pre-check list would have caught the broken button
- Structured rounds reduce overhead each time
